div.p: To all sons of Holy Mother Church present and to come, William of Lilliesleaf sends greeting. Know me to have given, granted, and by this my present charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, in pure and perpetual alms, two tofts on the western side of Haddington; to be held, had, and possessed as seems best and most useful to them, as freely, quitly, fully and honourably as they hold and possess any alms freely, quitly, fully and honourably; so that they shall give me and my heirs four pence at the Haddington fair the from those tofts annually. I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant to the said monks the aforementioned two tofts against all men, and discharge for ever every service and all other matters in perpetuity. With these witnesses: Richard le Naym; Walter of Lindsay; Henry son of Robert of Roxburgh; Robert of Barnoldby; Alexander of Lilliesleaf, and Geoffrey his brother; Ralph Spendlove, provost of Haddington; William the clerk; William Russell.
